extra.reasoning: The property is monotonicity of acuity with respect to the input `vomiting`: setting vomiting true while holding all other inputs fixed must never lower acuity. Examining all 12 rules (r1-r12), none of them reference `vomiting` as a condition. The rules' conditions involve only the other inputs: back_pain, confusion, focal_weakness, syncope, stiff_neck, fever, leg_swelling, vision_loss, chest_pain, hemoptysis, dyspnea, abdominal_pain. The only output is `acuity`, set by these rules with priority-based conflict resolution and a routine default for unset outputs. Since `vomiting` appears in no rule condition and there is no other mechanism (no default depends on it, no state), the acuity output is completely invariant under changing vomiting from false to true with all other inputs held constant. An unchanged acuity is trivially "no lower than before," so the monotonicity property holds for the entire input space (vacuously/by invariance). No counterexample exists.
